heading: Important Assets
content: Core NPU/edge-inference tooling repos: llama.cpp-npu, EdgeAIApp-ExecuTorch, mlc-llm, genymotion-device-web-player, off-grid-ai-mobile, qcom-build-utils, snapdragon-npu-llm, docker-pkg-build. All confirmed already forked under the c10vis-poem GitHub account.

heading: Important On-Device Layers (will be running)
content: Repos the user will actually run on-device: tmux, claude-code-android, termux-packages, OmniRoute, tmux-assistant-resurrect.

heading: Very Important: Proof of Concept, Reverse Engineering, Troubleshoot Fallback, Research Guide
content: off-grid-ai-mobile, VarientDemoApplication, Magisk. User note on Genymobile: this is Genymobile, her entire repo and website deserve scraping, this will not be the last time you hear me mention her or see a resource of hers posted, if I were you I would task agents of scrubbing her entire online and GitHub libraries -- flagged as an open follow-up task, not yet acted on. edge-overlay-ai and nexa-sdk: user's own assessment is I really don't think this works anymore but it could be worth looking at for reverse engineering and maybe it does support some active API key but I dk, matching the independent live-verification finding that Nexa's Hugging Face presence is effectively abandoned. Also scrcpy.

heading: Skills -- Don't Sleep On These
content: SuperClaude Framework, Claude-skills, leaked-system-prompts, Prompt-eng-interactive-tutorial, claude-code-best-practice. Highlighted as probably the most important skill, teaching Claude how to reverse engineer Android apps: claude-android-skill, android-reverse-engineering-skill.

heading: Voice and Memory Layers
content: Most likely get the version best suited to match, not sure of its tflite: moonshine-tflite, silero-vad, Kokoro. Memory layer: OB1, reasoning-bank.

heading: Cross-Reference: Repo READMEs Already Condensed Individually
content: Several repos from this list have dedicated reference docs elsewhere in this wiki, sourced from README exports found in the ANDROID APK/RESEARCH DOCS Drive folder -- the text reminders per fork repo the user referred to, a set of numbered README (N) (Markor) files, one per repo. qcom-build-utils is centralized build tooling for the Qualcomm Linux Debian package ecosystem with reusable GitHub Actions workflows, ABI checking, and package promotion/release pipelines. snapdragon-npu-llm runs real LLMs on Hexagon NPU including the unsupported Snapdragon 8 Gen 1 (Hexagon v69), built on a K9FxNa Hugging Face .pte artifact plus ExecuTorch QNN backend, verified 31.3 tok/s on a OnePlus 10 Pro. silero-vad is a pre-trained enterprise-grade Voice Activity Detector, MIT license, ONNX/PyTorch, sub-1ms per 30ms audio chunk on CPU, 8kHz/16kHz sampling, no telemetry/keys/lock-in. off-grid-ai-mobile is the app on the user's phone referenced earlier in this session (model browser with storage indicator and local-server auto-discovery), a full offline AI suite for Android/iOS/macOS running Qwen 3/Llama 3.2/Gemma 3/Phi-4/any GGUF, discovering local OpenAI-compatible servers automatically (Ollama, LM Studio, LocalAI), with on-device Stable Diffusion, Whisper STT, and an on-device embedding plus cosine retrieval knowledge base in SQLite. android-reverse-engineering-skill is a Claude Code skill (Apache-2.0) that decompiles APK/XAPK/JAR/AAR via jadx/Fernflower/Vineflower and extracts HTTP APIs, traces call flows, and handles ProGuard/R8 obfuscation. EdgeAIApp-ExecuTorch runs on-device CLIP via ExecuTorch plus Qualcomm QNN backend on Android for zero-shot image classification, about 100-150ms inference, about 800MB RAM, requiring Snapdragon 8 Gen 2/3/Elite (QNN v79).

heading: Relevance to This Project
content: This doc is the master index for the GitHub repo-scraping phase of the project. Its content maps directly onto Omni Claw's execution layers: the on-device layers section (tmux, termux-packages, OmniRoute, claude-code-android) matches the blueprint's Local Shell Loop; the voice and memory layers section (silero-vad, Kokoro, OB1, reasoning-bank) matches the Local Knowledge Synthesis backend already formalized in the integration architecture doc; and off-grid-ai-mobile is a strong working precedent for Omni Claw's own model-browser/local-server-discovery UX, since its Remote LLM Servers feature (auto-discover OpenAI-compatible servers on the local network) is effectively what Omni Claw's OmniRoute integration and cloud-fallback routing would need to replicate.
